author: jasonmotylinski
description: Displays backlinks in a tree structure.
name: Hierarchical Backlinks

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### ✨ New feature
- **Supercharged Links-style coloring for backlink rows** (Closes #3). A new opt-in setting — **Settings → Styling → "Supercharged Links attributes"** (off by default) — decorates each backlink row with the same `data-link-*` attributes (and `--data-link-*` CSS variables) that Supercharged Links applies, derived from each note's frontmatter and tags. Your existing Supercharged Links CSS snippets then style the hierarchical backlinks, with **no dependency on the Supercharged Links plugin**.

  Example snippet:
  ```css
  .tree-item-inner[data-link-status="done"]  { color: var(--text-muted); }
  .tree-item-inner[data-link-tags*="urgent"] { color: var(--color-red); }
  ```

  Note: because rows are `div.tree-item-inner` elements, snippets scoped to `a.internal-link[...]` should target `.tree-item-inner[...]` (or be left unscoped).

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jasonmotylinski/hierarchical-backlinks/compare/1.12.0...1.13.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### 🐛 Fixes
- **Collapse Tree no longer hides a folder note's own backlinks** (Fixes #167). On a merged folder-note row — where the folder note itself has backlinks shown inline — "Collapse Tree" used to hide those results along with the folder's subtree, because both shared a single collapse state. "Collapse Tree" and "Collapse Results" now act independently on folder-note rows.

### ✨ Improvements
- **"Collapse tree" button is now hidden while Flatten tree is active** (Closes #168). It has no effect in flatten mode (every row is a leaf), so it's hidden to reduce clutter.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jasonmotylinski/hierarchical-backlinks/compare/1.11.2...1.12.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug fixes

- **Flatten tree no longer shows ancestor folder notes** (#154) — folder notes
  injected only to make folder rows clickable no longer appear as standalone
  rows (with 0 matches) in flatten mode. Folder notes that are actual backlinks
  still appear.
- **Whole-row clicks work in flatten mode** (#155) — clicking anywhere on a
  leaf row now opens the note, matching the behavior of merged folder-note
  rows in hierarchy mode.
- **Collapse tree no longer collapses results** (#145) — "Collapse tree" now
  only collapses folder rows; result matches keep following the separate
  "Collapse results" toggle.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug fixes

- **Outer folders with an indexed folder note are now clickable.** When `folderNoteIndexName` is configured (e.g. "Overview"), parent folders whose folder note has no backlinks of its own were not being injected into the hierarchy, so clicking the folder name did nothing. The injection logic now mirrors the render-layer strategy: indexed-name first, falling back to same-name. Closes #144.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New features

- **Click a backlink match snippet to jump to that exact line in the source file.** Previously, clicking opened the file at the top. Per-line match snippets now navigate via Obsidian's `eState` line/column hint, mirroring the native backlinks pane. Clicks on the row container still open the file at the top, and frontmatter-property matches keep the file-level open since their positions aren't file offsets. Closes #128.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug fixes

- **Collapse Results now works correctly when Collapse Tree is active.** Previously, toggling Collapse Results off would only expand top-level result blocks; nested results stayed collapsed because their folder ancestor was collapsed. The leaf's expanded state is now independent of folder visibility, mirroring the Collapse-On behavior. Fixes #147.
- **Collapse Results no longer breaks after activating Flatten Tree on a folder note.** Same root cause as #147 manifesting via the flatten path: a flattened leaf's reference to its original folder-note parent caused the toggle-off to be skipped. Fixes #146.

Closes #146, #147.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

- Fixed #112: Folder notes without direct backlinks are now clickable in the hierarchy tree. When a folder note (e.g., `Project/Project.md`) exists but has no direct backlinks, it's now properly included in the backlinks hierarchy, making it accessible in the tree view even when only child files have backlinks.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
New Features (by @bitbalancer):                   
                                                                            
  1. **Collapse controls** - Collapse all top-level nodes or hide context of leaf notes                                                                
  2. **Flatten mode** - Show only leaf nodes (files) without folder hierarchy   
  3. **Sort order** - Toggle reverse alphabetical ordering                      
  4. **Search/filter** - Filter backlinks using Obsidian's search syntax (supports key:value, [prop], quoted phrases, negation, OR, regex)         
  5. **Lock** - Freeze the tree so it persists when switching notes             
  6. **Hotkey support** - All toggle buttons have assignable hotkeys            
  7. **Setting** - Option to hide context by default
